objective: Strengthen backend IO coordination and token-efficiency telemetry in the codex-orch-unified swarm runtime, with a compact diagnostics surface in the existing dashboard.

changes.summary: Added typed IO coordinator snapshot models and default state factory.
Added IO coordinator state updates and transient SSE broadcasts without polluting the visible event log.
Refactored the coordinator into a stateful telemetry collector tracking retries, latency, failures, active calls, and context-compression savings.
Registered IO telemetry publishing into swarm state and reset coordinator metrics at run start.
Added an IO diagnostics panel showing retries, latency, failure state, and estimated token savings.

outcome: Backend IO coordination now publishes typed runtime metrics into the swarm state, exposing retry pressure and estimated context savings needed to control token costs in multi-agent runs.
